_comment: SeaweedFS S3 IAM config for local development. Three identities: learn-api-rw has full access on both buckets; tusd-upload can only write/list/head objects under learn-uploads/; anonymous can read learn-vod/ only so that nginx (which proxies signed /hls/ URLs to SeaweedFS without S3 credentials) can serve HLS playlists + segments. HLS-level auth is enforced by nginx secure_link upstream of SeaweedFS, not by SeaweedFS itself. The keys here MUST match the docker-compose defaults so the out-of-the-box local stack works with no extra substitution step. When we tackle deploy, this file gets rendered with real credentials sourced from whatever secrets store we pick then.
